title: taraxacum horridum
intro: Taraxacum horridum, also known as the rough dandelion, is a plant species belonging to the sunflower family, Asteraceae, and is often mistaken for the common dandelion (Taraxacum officinale). It is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant's leaves, roots, and flowers have been used to treat a variety of health issues, such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, and have been shown to have health benefits like reducing inflammation, improving liver function, and boosting the immune system. Taraxacum horridum can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as a tea, used topically as a salve, or taken as a dietary supplement.
